customManagers.description: M1 (Option A): ComposeApp image=/version= pairs. image= MUST sit directly above version= (only comments/whitespace between). caddy-custom is skipped automatically because its version is an unquoted variable (version=caddy_version), so the quoted-version match never fires -- its base image is tracked by M2 instead.

M2: the caddy base image behind the locally-built caddy-custom (docker.build in proxies/__init__.py uses caddy:${CADDY_VERSION}). Tracks the upstream caddy tag on Docker Hub; bumping caddy_version rebuilds the custom image.

M3: secondary/sidecar images pinned inline in compose templates (redis, gotenberg, tika, node-exporter, snmp-exporter, cadvisor). Requires depName to start [a-z0-9] so the templated 'image: [[ app.image ]]:[[ app.version ]]' lines are never matched.

M4: grafana/loki-docker-driver plugin (docker.plugin). Uses an arch-suffixed tag (X.Y.Z-amd64) because the driver publishes no multi-arch tag; the regex versioning restricts candidates to -amd64 tags and compares them by semver.

packageRules.description: LinuxServer.io images carry a build suffix (e.g. 5.0.4-ls261); keep their PRs grouped and clearly labelled since the upstream-version part is what matters.
apache/tika pins the -full variant (3.3.1.0-full); restrict candidate tags to that variant so Renovate doesn't propose the slim tag.

The forgejo-runner's docker-in-docker image pins the -dind variant (e.g. 28.3.3-dind). The regex versioning already ignores non -dind tags; allowedVersions is an explicit belt-and-suspenders guard so a bare tag like `29` can never be proposed (which would drop DinD). Major -dind bumps (e.g. 29.x-dind) are still offered for review.
